title: Game Theory Expert: America WILL Lose Iran War! - Professor Jiang
full_text: Hi guys, we're only a few days in, but it looks like the United States is going to lose the war in Iran. Now, that's according to Professor Yang, who is not a historian, not a military expert, not even an economist. He's actually an expert in game theory. Now, he was interviewed by Breaking Points on YouTube. And what he had to say here about how this is all going to go down is quite interesting, to put it mildly. Have a listen. I wanted to show folks that back in uh 2024, you made three big predictions. One, that Trump would win. Two, that he would start a war with Iran. And three, that the US would lose that war. Let's take a listen to that. >> In this class this semester, I'm making three big predictions, right? First is that Trump will win uh in November. Second is that um United States will go to war against Iran. And the third big prediction is that the United States will lose this war which will forever change the global order. >> So obviously that last one is uh the only one who is that has not yet come to fruition is quite a stunning prediction. You know do you stand by it and what have you seen so far that leads you to sort of stand firm in that conclusion? So um given my analysis of how the war is progressing, I think that Iran has many more advantages over the United States. Um the reality is that right now um it's a war of attrition between the United States and Iran. And the Iranians have preparing 20 years for this conflict. In their esquetology, in their religion, this is a war against the great Satan. They've had many um practice runs. Last June was a 12-day war when the Iranians were able to uh examine analyze the strike capacities of both the Israelis and the Americans. >> And he's and he's completely correct here. So, he was right about Trump winning. He was right about the United States going to war with Iran. and he was right about how the Iranians have been preparing for a long time, 20 years, for an attack like this and they're ready even though it seems the United States isn't. Now, he went on to talk about how this is going to affect the global economy as well. >> So, what uh the Iranians are doing is they're not they're waging war against the entire global economy. And so, they are striking um the GCC countries. And not only are they striking GC countries, American bases, they're going after the critical energy infrastructure of these bases. They blocked off the tropical moves and eventually um they will go after the water dissalidation plants which is a lifeblood of these nations because they don't have fresh water supply. In fact, uh the water desalination plant provides 60% of the um GCC's water supply. M so if a drone and you know these drones cost $50,000. If they wiped out a disselation plant in Riyad uh Saudi Arabia and it's a city of 10 million people, right? Um they'd be out of water in two weeks. >> Just think about that. Saudi Arabia running out of water in two weeks. Dubai running out of water in a number of days or weeks as well. So this is this would make a lot of sense from the uranian point of view. you attack these countries, they're soft targets. You know, the ones that are really protected are of course Israel and some of the other Arab nations. But if you look at the amount of missiles that are being launched at the UAE, the UAE is a bigger target than Israel is. So why would Iran be attacking the UAE more than Israel? because it wants to undermine the Gulf nations, the ones that are aligned with the United States. They they want to they see, okay, if we attack these countries, they're going to put pressure on the United States to to stop because they are the ones who are being who are not so protected. They don't have the defenses that Israel has, and they're in a position where they can pressure or attempt to pressure the United States to stop >> um in two weeks. And right now the Iranians have de facto closed off the shumoose and the GCC gets 90% of its food from um uh the Humus. So I know a lot of people are talking about the disruptions to global economy but right now the uh Iranians are actually threatening the very existence of Saudi Arabia um UAE Bahrain uh Qatar and what this is important is that uh the Gulf states are really the lynch pin of the um American economy. So what they do is they sell petro dollars and then they recycle the petro dollars back into the American economy through the investments in the stock market. And right now we know that the entire glob the entire American economy is propped up by AI investments in data centers and a lot of that comes from uh the Gulf States. So if um the uh Gulf states are no longer able to sell oil and they're no [clears throat] longer able to finance uh AI uh this AI bubble in the United States, then this AI AI will burst and with it will will um uh will burst as well as well the entire American economy which is >> this is insane. I I leave a link in the description of this video so you can watch the entire interview. It goes on for about 15 minutes but I and I do recommend watching it but this is insane. So what we're seeing is Iran is not able to defeat Israel. It's not a able to defeat the United States. It's not even able to reach the United States. So what is it doing instead? It's pumping rockets into soft targets in the Middle East, undermining these nations, blocking trade, trade in food and and oil that is necessary for these economies to exist. If these economies collapse, then they're no longer uh engaging with the United States and the United States is in trouble. But it's not just the United States, it's the global economy. So, I think what we're going to see, and Professor Yang also predicts this, that the United States will be forced into a position where they'll have to capitulate or they'll go into a situation where they put boots on the ground. Now, the Secretary of State for War has ruled this out for the moment. So, no, no, we're not we're not even considering the idea of putting boots on the ground, but the United States may be forced into a position into a position where they have to completely defeat the Iranians. Like, you you're not going to win this with air power alone. There have been other situations in the past where air power was sufficient, but it's not going to be sufficient when it comes to Iran because you have a massive military there and defeating the air for the air force and uh knocking out a few targets on the ground is not going to be sufficient. If you want regime change, you need to have troops on the ground. So, what we're probably going to see is a capitulation of the United States and say, "Look, we we'll we'll stop bombing you and leave you in place." Or you're going to see a massive military buildup and American and maybe other countries going along with the United States into Iran to force a change. But this is going to be extremely damaging to the world economy and it's going to be extremely damaging to the reputation of the United States. Donald Trump won on the back of I'm not I'm not going into foreign conflicts. I'm going to be a peace president. And he's acting pretty much like George W. Bush. So once again, I I recommend watching the entire interview. It's really interesting. But as I said at the beginning, it's important to take everything that everything that he says with a pinch of salt because while he has been correct about a lot of issues, uh we can't use this as a predictor for the future. Let me know in the comment section, guys, what you think, and I'll see you in the next video. If you've made it this far, thank you so much for watching this video. Remember to click like, subscribe, and ring the bell in order to stay in the loop for future videos. Now, you can also support the channel financially by clicking on the links below to Patreon, becoming a member, and of course, sending me a coffee. And all of those are greatly appreciated, but of course, only if you're able to. Remember, I stream twice a week on Tuesday and Friday nights. Uh, and I hope to see you on the streams as well. So, have a great day and I'll see you in the next video.
